message:
feat(demo): data viz screen and scorer updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -568,16 +568,18 @@
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","title":"Define diff cost model + Bayesian decision policy","description":"Background
- We can choose between diff strategies per frame: full diff (`BufferDiff::compute`), dirty-row diff (`compute_dirty`), or full redraw.
- Choice should be based on expected cost, not ad-hoc thresholds.

Task
- Specify a cost model for diff+emit: cost = c_scan * N_cells_scanned + c_emit * N_cells_changed + c_overhead.
- Define how to estimate change-rate posterior `p` from observed dirty rows or changed cells.
- Define expected cost for each strategy and the decision rule: pick argmin expected cost.

Details
- Posterior: p ~ Beta(α, β). Update each frame using observed change fraction (dirty rows or changed cells).
- Use `E[p]` and optional upper-quantile (conservative) for worst-case inlining.
- Decide when to fall back to full redraw (e.g., p above threshold or buffer dimension mismatch).

Acceptance Criteria
- Written spec in issue description is sufficient to implement without extra docs.
- Decision rule is deterministic and uses only past data and current buffer metadata.

Notes
- Keep the model simple (O(1) update) to avoid adding overhead.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:35.634169961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:35.634169961Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:35.634169961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","title":"Implement dirty-row diff path + dirty state lifecycle","description":"Background
- `Buffer` tracks dirty rows and `BufferDiff::compute_dirty` exists.
- Runtime currently uses full diff in `TerminalWriter::present_*`.

Task
- Integrate `compute_dirty` into the presentation path while preserving output correctness.
- Establish dirty-row lifecycle: when to clear dirty flags so subsequent frames are correct.

Implementation Notes
- Use dirty rows from the new frame (the buffer produced by view/render).
- After a diff is computed and emitted, clear dirty flags on the new buffer before storing as prev.
- Handle dimension mismatch by forcing full diff and marking all dirty.

Acceptance Criteria
- Output equivalence proven via unit tests (dirty diff vs full diff).
- No regressions in inline or alt-screen.

Risks
- Forgetting to clear dirty rows could cause under-diffing later. Tests must guard.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:47.490619370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:16.873182673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:47.490619370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:16.873133570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","title":"Implement change-rate posterior (Beta) + expected-cost evaluation","description":"Background
- We need a lightweight online estimator to decide among diff strategies.

Task
- Implement Beta posterior updates for change rate `p`.
- Provide expected-cost functions for each diff strategy using the cost model from A1.
- Store minimal state inside `TerminalWriter` (or adjacent module) with deterministic updates.

Considerations
- Use dirty-row fraction as a proxy for change fraction when full diff is avoided.
- Optionally update posterior using actual changed-cell fraction when full diff is computed.
- Clamp p to avoid degenerate 0/1 extremes; keep α,β >= ε.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic updates, no allocations in hot path.
- Decision can be logged with contributing terms (p estimate, costs).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:53.638516835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.070706600Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:53.638516835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.070665593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","title":"Wire strategy selection into TerminalWriter (inline + alt)","description":"Background
- After implementing dirty-row diff and change-rate posterior, we must integrate a unified decision point in presentation.

Task
- Add a strategy selector in `TerminalWriter` that chooses: full diff, dirty diff, or full redraw, based on expected cost and safe constraints.
- Ensure selection is identical for inline and alt-screen paths.

Constraints
- Must remain deterministic; no randomness.
- Must handle dimension changes by forcing full diff/redraw.
- Keep changes localized to `TerminalWriter` to avoid API churn unless needed.

Acceptance Criteria
- One decision path used for both modes.
- Logs include chosen strategy + cost breakdown (for evidence ledger).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.461469739Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.266959121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.461428983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","title":"Wire strategy selection into TerminalWriter (inline + alt)","description":"Background
- After implementing dirty-row diff and change-rate posterior, we must integrate a unified decision point in presentation.

Task
- Add a strategy selector in `TerminalWriter` that chooses: full diff, dirty diff, or full redraw, based on expected cost and safe constraints.
- Ensure selection is identical for inline and alt-screen paths.

Constraints
- Must remain deterministic; no randomness.
- Must handle dimension changes by forcing full diff/redraw.
- Keep changes localized to `TerminalWriter` to avoid API churn unless needed.

Acceptance Criteria
- One decision path used for both modes.
- Logs include chosen strategy + cost breakdown (for evidence ledger).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:27.853605950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.266959121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.461428983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:27.853557198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.5","title":"Tests: dirty diff soundness + strategy equivalence","description":"Background
- Dirty-row diff and strategy selection must never change output.

Task
- Add unit/property tests to verify:
  - `compute_dirty` output == `compute` output when dirty invariant holds.
  - Dirty flags are correctly cleared after present.
  - Strategy selection chooses a valid strategy and does not change output.

Suggested Locations
- `crates/ftui-render/tests/no_flicker_proof.rs` or new tests in `ftui-render`.
- `crates/ftui-runtime/tests/` for end-to-end diff decision behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover both inline and alt-screen modes where relevant.
- All tests deterministic and pass under `cargo test`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:06.691336337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.661391175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:06.691336337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.661345078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.1.6","title":"Perf: large-screen diff microbench + regressions","description":"Background
- Performance gains should be measurable on large screens and sparse updates.

Task
- Add a microbench or test harness to compare full diff vs dirty diff on large buffers (e.g., 200x60, 240x80).
- Record timings and basic counters; keep deterministic.

Acceptance Criteria
- Test fails on obvious regressions (e.g., dirty diff slower than full diff beyond tolerance).
- Output format is JSONL or structured logs to integrate with CI later.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:11.616472862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.863661508Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:11.616472862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:17.863606144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","title":"API: expose diff strategy config + defaults","description":"Background
- API changes are allowed; strategy selection should be configurable for experimentation.

Task
- Add a `DiffStrategyConfig` (or similar) to runtime config with:
  - enable/disable Bayesian selection
  - cost model constants
  - dirty-row usage toggle
- Define safe defaults that preserve current behavior or improve it conservatively.

Acceptance Criteria
- Config is documented and wired into `TerminalWriter`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:21.282939455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:27.649974051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:21.282939455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:27.649911063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2","title":"BOCPD Resize Coalescer Upgrade","description":"Background
- Resize coalescer currently uses a heuristic burst/steady regime with hysteresis thresholds.
- We want a mathematically principled, adaptive regime detector with formal properties.

Goal
- Replace heuristic regime detection with Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection (BOCPD) to classify steady vs burst behavior.
- Preserve existing invariants: latest-wins, bounded latency, deterministic decisions.

Key Concepts
- Run-length posterior over regime changes, hazard function for change probability.
- Decision uses posterior mass over burst regime to choose coalescing delay.
- Computation kept O(K) with run-length truncation.

Success Criteria
- Coalescer remains deterministic and respects hard deadlines.
- Fewer wasted renders during resize storms with no additional latency in steady mode.
- Decision logs include posterior summary for auditability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","title":"BOCPD design: hazard, run-length cap, decision rule","description":"Background
- We need a formal regime detector for resize bursts that replaces heuristic thresholds.

Task
- Define BOCPD model: observation = inter-arrival time or event rate; hazard function H(r);
  predictive likelihood for steady vs burst regimes; run-length truncation K for O(K) update.
- Specify decision rule: use posterior mass on burst regime to select coalescing delay.

Details
- Choose observation model (e.g., exponential for inter-arrival, or Poisson for counts).
- Hazard: constant hazard or adaptive based on recent rate.
- Provide defaults tuned for responsiveness on large terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Design doc in issue description is implementable as-is.
- Explicit complexity bounds and deterministic update rules.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:24.375476337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:24.375476337Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:24.375476337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","title":"Implement BOCPD state + update logic in resize coalescer","description":"Background
- BOCPD requires maintaining a run-length posterior and updating each resize event.

Task
- Implement BOCPD update step inside `resize_coalescer.rs` (or a small internal struct).
- Keep memory bounded with run-length truncation.

Constraints
- Deterministic updates, no floating-point nondeterminism beyond standard f64.
- No allocations in hot path beyond bounded buffers.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests confirm posterior sums to ~1 (within epsilon) and update is stable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:29.847358657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:54.823368012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:29.847358657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:54.823321194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","title":"Integrate BOCPD into coalescer decision path","description":"Background
- Existing coalescer uses steady/burst thresholds. We will replace or augment with BOCPD regime posterior.

Task
- Use BOCPD posterior to decide between steady vs burst delays.
- Preserve invariants: latest-wins, bounded latency, determinism, hard deadline.

Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior matches or improves existing coalescer under burst and steady tests.
- Fallback path exists if BOCPD is disabled in config.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:34.663541982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.017961166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:34.663541982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.017902235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","title":"Integrate BOCPD into coalescer decision path","description":"Background
- Existing coalescer uses steady/burst thresholds. We will replace or augment with BOCPD regime posterior.

Task
- Use BOCPD posterior to decide between steady vs burst delays.
- Preserve invariants: latest-wins, bounded latency, determinism, hard deadline.

Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior matches or improves existing coalescer under burst and steady tests.
- Fallback path exists if BOCPD is disabled in config.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:34.663541982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:38.993596050Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:34.663541982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.017902235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:38.993538532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.4","title":"Evidence ledger: log BOCPD posterior + decision rationale","description":"Background
- BOCPD decisions should be explainable and auditable.

Task
- Extend JSONL decision logs to include: run-length posterior summary, burst probability, chosen delay, and whether hard deadline forced.
- Keep logging optional via config.

Acceptance Criteria
- Logs are deterministic and parseable; include a stable schema version.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:39.401151700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.219358694Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:39.401151700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.219322826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.5","title":"Tests: BOCPD coalescer invariants + burst/steady scenarios","description":"Background
- BOCPD integration must preserve invariants and improve behavior.

Task
- Add unit tests for:
  - Latest-wins (final resize always applied)
  - Bounded latency (hard deadline)
  - Determinism (same event sequence => same decisions)
  - Burst vs steady classification correctness in synthetic sequences

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests live in `crates/ftui-runtime/tests/` or `resize_coalescer` module tests.
- Must pass under `cargo test`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:45.032084227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.419042502Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:45.032084227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.419003428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.2.6","title":"Simulation: compare BOCPD vs heuristic coalescer","description":"Background
- We want evidence that BOCPD improves coalescing behavior without regressions.

Task
- Add a simulation test that feeds synthetic resize streams (steady, burst, oscillatory) to both coalescers and compares latency + render count.
- Keep deterministic; report results as JSONL or structured logs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Simulation highlights reduced render count during burst with bounded latency.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:50.034448481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.617400882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:50.034448481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:55.617352310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.2.7","title":"API: BOCPD config + fallback heuristic toggle","description":"Background
- We need explicit control of BOCPD parameters and a fallback path for comparison.

Task
- Add config fields to resize coalescer config:
  - enable_bocpd (bool)
  - hazard parameters, run-length cap
  - observation model parameters
- Ensure fallback to current heuristic when disabled.

Acceptance Criteria
- Config is wired, documented, and deterministic.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:34.570366835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:38.782419803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:34.570366835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:38.782364419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.3","title":"Conformal Frame-Time Predictor for Budget","description":"Background
- Budget controller currently uses PID + e-process to decide degrade/upgrade.
- We want distribution-free risk control for frame-time overruns, especially on large screens.

Goal
- Add a conformal predictor that provides (1-α) coverage for frame-time risk and gates degradation decisions.
- Keep behavior deterministic, light-weight, and compatible with existing budget controller.

Key Concepts
- Nonconformity score based on frame-time residuals.
- Sliding window or exponential decay to adapt to drift.
- Quantile-based threshold to ensure coverage without distribution assumptions.

Success Criteria
- Empirically validate coverage in simulation tests.
- No regressions in render cadence; degrade decisions become more reliable under heavy load.
- Configurable α with safe defaults.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:20.724471596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:20.724471596Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:20.724471596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.3.1","title":"Conformal predictor design for frame-time risk","description":"Background
- We need a distribution-free method to bound frame-time overruns.

Task
- Specify conformal prediction setup:
  - Nonconformity score (e.g., |frame_time - target| or positive overrun).
  - Sliding window or exponential decay for adaptivity.
  - Quantile computation and finite-sample correction.
- Define how the predictor gates degrade/upgrade decisions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear formulae and update rules with complexity bounds.
- Parameter defaults suitable for large screens.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:18:02.172993187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:18:02.172993187Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:18:02.172993187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.3.2","title":"Implement conformal predictor module + state","description":"Background
- Conformal predictor needs a small state machine and quantile calculator.

Task
- Implement predictor (likely in `ftui-render/src/budget.rs` or adjacent module).
- Support bounded memory (window cap) and deterministic quantile computation.
